danger_signals.visual_form: Health bar or numeric display (unused score display code suggests alternative UI considered)
danger_signals.meaning: Player approaching death state; world becoming hostile to survival

danger_signals.visual_form: Level-specific obstacle sprites and environmental collision

safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety enhancement or health restoration
Distance from immediate threat; advancement toward objective

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Terminator unit sprites; varying hostile AI archetypes across stages
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hospital level with graffiti (unused prototype feature) suggesting infiltration and control of human structures
Player character positioning and level design implies survival narrative; hospital as contested human sanctuary

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Skynet threat active / Judgment Day imminent
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Terminator enemy presence in human environments (hospital); hostile architecture and obstacles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must navigate platforming challenges while evading enemies; level design enforces evasion/combat pressure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hospital level layout; graffiti marks on walls (prototype graphics still in ROM, suggesting marking of territory or sabotage)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player navigates corridors as if through enemy-controlled space; survival platforming mechanics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unused downbeat jingle in ROM suggests audio-visual transition state; main BGM continues in released version (incomplete implementation)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continue/retry prompt; score display unavailable in final build despite code support

environmental_storytelling.example: Hospital stage demonstrates Skynet control over human infrastructure; graffiti in prototype version marks contested space and AI presence
Level geometry and enemy placement communicate world-state of human evasion through hostile territory

environmental_storytelling.technique: Unused audio cue for world state
environmental_storytelling.example: Downbeat jingle intended for death/Game Over sequence would audibly mark failure state; absence in final version suggests incomplete world-state communication

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Sprite animation and collision detection drive player evasion mechanics; threat presence alters safe traversal paths

entity_behavioral_tells.visual_behavior: Health bar depletion (or alternative score display mode via RAM manipulation) signals critical world-state transition toward failure

notes: Source material (TCRF) documents development history and unused assets but contains minimal explicit information on visual communication systems for danger/safety/faction identity. Extraction relies on inferred game design from platformer paradigm, Terminator IP context, and remnant code/graphics. Hospital level with graffiti (unused prototype graphics) and intended high-score UI suggest designers planned richer world-state visualization than final build delivered. Incomplete audio implementation (unused jingle, continued BGM on death) indicates manifestation layer was partially implemented.
